zh.lifeSummary: Hélène ou Élisabeth du Donjon de Corbeil是Robert FitzEdith of Normandy和Matilda d'Avranches的女兒，於大約1137年出生在英國英格蘭戴文奧可哈普頓。她和Sir Renaud de Courtenay I至少有3個兒子和2個女兒。她去世於1224年享年88歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Corbeil
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Jacques, Fernand, Jean-Louis, Armand, Fernande, Gilles, Hugues, Huguette, Jean Pierre, Jean-Baptiste, Leonie.

French: from Old French corbeil ‘basket’ (from Late Latin corbicula, a diminutive of corbis ‘basket’), a metonymic occupational name for a maker and seller of baskets, or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word because of a depression in the ground. Alternatively, a nickname derived from Old French corb ‘raven’ (see Corp ). Compare Curby and Kirby .
Altered form of French (mainly Loire-Atlantique) Gourbil, a surname which is most likely derived from the same Old French words corbeil ‘basket’ or corb ‘raven’ as the name in 1 above.

variants.name: Curby
meanings: Americanized form of French   Corbeil  .
English: variant of   Corby  .

variants.name: Corp
meanings: English (mainly Somerset): in the southwest from Old French corp ‘raven’, probably applied as a nickname for someone with glossy dark hair. In northern and eastern (Scandinavian-influenced) counties the name may alternatively be derived from the cognate Old Norse korpr.
French: nickname from Old French corp ‘raven’ (see 1 above).

nameMeanings.name: Hélène
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   Helen  , now in fairly frequent use in the English-speaking world, usually without the accents.

variants.name: Helena
meanings: form of   Helen  , used also in Germany, the Netherlands, Scandinavia, and Eastern Europe.

nameMeanings.name: ou
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: ‘Wealthy’.

nameMeanings.name: Élisabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

variants.name: Elizabeth
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

nameMeanings.name: du
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: ‘Wealthy’.

nameMeanings.name: Donjon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Lincolnshire and Nottinghamshire):
perhaps a shortened form of   Donaldson   or   Dunstan  , or a perhaps a patronymic from a shortened form of the latter.
(of French Huguenot origin): altered form of Dansan, a habitational name, with fused preposition d(e) ‘from’, denoting someone from Ansan (Gers).

variants.name: Donaldson
meanings: Scottish: patronymic from Donald , generally representing an Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Dhomhnaill (see McDonald ).

en.lifeSummary: Hélène ou Élisabeth du Donjon de Corbeil was born about 1137, in Okehampton, Devon, England, United Kingdom as the daughter of Robert FitzEdith of Normandy and Matilda d'Avranches. She had at least 3 sons and 2 daughters with Sir Renaud de Courtenay I. She died in 1224, at the age of 88.

fr.lifeSummary: Hélène ou Élisabeth du Donjon de Corbeil est née environ 1137, à Okehampton, Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, fille de Robert FitzEdith of Normandy et Matilda d'Avranches. Elle a eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles avec Sir Renaud de Courtenay I. Elle est décédée en 1224, à l’âge de 88 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Hélène ou Élisabeth du Donjon de Corbeil(은)는 대략 1137년 영국 잉글랜드 데폰 오케함프턴에서 Robert FitzEdith of Normandy(와)과 Matilda d'Avranches의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 Sir Renaud de Courtenay I(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1224년 88세의 나이로 사망했다.
